title: Iran-US talks extend into second day as strait of Hormuz showdown deepens
subhead: The US military said Saturday that two Navy warships transited through the strait to begin clearing it of mines. The Iranian military denied that any American warships had entered the waterway and threatened to respond if they do so.

title: Lebanese mourn victims of 'Black Wednesday': 'We are not just numbers'

Taiwan's main opposition party aligns more closely with Beijing in the name of peace
subhead: The meeting between Chinese President Xi Jinping and Kuomintang leader Cheng Li-wun underscores that China 'would under no circumstances tolerate' the island's independence.

title: In Hungary, at the 'End the System' concert: 'This youth who care about politics, we've never seen anything like it'
subhead: Ahead of high-stakes legislative elections, tens of thousands of people gathered in Budapest to hear 50 music groups urge voters to go to the polls.

title: The Australian island that could be the key to koalas' survival
subhead: The small marsupial once seemed doomed to extinction on French Island due to inbreeding. Today, it boasts the most thriving population in Australia.

title: 'I stared death in the face!' On board the Trans-Siberian, a 9,000-kilometer journey through an exhausted Russia
subhead: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (1/2). In late December 2025 and early 2026, on the train linking Moscow to Vladivostok, soldiers returning from Ukraine cross paths with cautious travelers, careful about what they say. Beyond the Urals, propaganda posters attempt by every possible means to recruit new soldiers.

The planning battle to revive Tour Montparnasse, Paris's only skyscraper
subhead: After more than 10 years of negotiations between Paris City Hall and the tower's co-owners, the transformation of the now-empty Tour Montparnasse and its property complex is set to begin in autumn 2026.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Emeline Cazi and  Isabelle Regnier

title: Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
subhead: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

title: A French judge was found burned at a cliff in Djibouti in 1995. How and why he died remains a mystery
subhead: On October 19, 1995, the body of Bernard Borrel was found burned at the base of a cliff in Djibouti. More than 30 years later, his widow Elisabeth Borrel continues her fight to shed light on the case, which has become entangled in military interests, diplomatic pressure and state secrets.
